summary: Current-code crosscheck of llmwiki_two_integrated_reviews_actual_crosscheck_report_20260506 identified residual distribution sealing, external-report current ZIP binding, full-suite nodeid digest, and release workflow authority follow-ups without duplicating profile-matrix or typed-learning telemetry automation already recorded.

observations.surface: release closeout batch manifest, schema, Makefile release-evidence-closeout
observations.problem: The checked-in batch manifest reports sealed_clean_pass while distribution_package.status remains not_provided, so artifact sealing can still be read as clean even when no distribution ZIP is bound.
observations.why_it_matters: This preserves the core distribution-closure ambiguity identified by the crosscheck report: local evidence can look portable before source package identity and path/content parity are sealed.
observations.automation_candidate: Make release claim mode require a materialized distribution package or emit not_sealed/unknown, and update tests that currently expect sealed_clean_pass with no distribution ZIP.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-06: release-closeout-batch-manifest now reports unsealed_distribution_not_provided/invalid/drift unless distribution_package is materialized with path and content parity; operator-release-summary independently rejects sealed batch status without a materialized distribution package. Follow-up automated on 2026-05-07: Make now exposes release-distribution-zip plus release-evidence-closeout-sealed, staging the source package under build/release and passing it to batch manifest promote/verify as both distribution ZIP and ZIP metadata.

observations.surface: external report reference manifest and review closeout target
observations.problem: The script can compute current_distribution_zip from a ZIP path, but the Make target leaves it optional and the checked-in manifest still has an unspecified current distribution; the current root also has one more active Markdown report than the manifest references.
observations.why_it_matters: External review provenance remains stale unless the final packaging or review handoff always regenerates the manifest after the actual ZIP and active report set are known.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a strict review/release mode that requires --current-distribution-zip-path, records basis/current mismatch, and fails if root external report references drift from the current file set.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-06: external-report-reference-manifest now has advisory and strict_review_release modes; strict mode requires --current-distribution-zip-path, distribution_provenance exposes basis/current ZIP match status and mismatch fields, and active_reference_set records prior/current root report count drift.

observations.problem: Full-suite evidence exists and passes, but collect-nodeids skips selector-free pytest commands, leaving the full-suite nodeid digest empty.
observations.why_it_matters: A 944-passed artifact is much harder to audit or replay without a collected nodeid set, even when the suite count matches the current tests.
observations.automation_candidate: Collect all test nodeids for selector-free full-suite commands with pytest --collect-only -q, then assert consistency between collected count and pass/skip/xfail totals.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-07: test_execution_summary collect-nodeids now runs pytest --collect-only -q even for selector-free full-suite commands, records nodeid_outcome_consistency, and the full-suite Make target captures JUnit XML plus execution-log digests.

observations.surface: GitHub release workflow and release evidence artifacts
observations.problem: The publish job still reads checked-in release-closeout-summary.json after a fresh checkout instead of consuming a live verified gate artifact from the verify job.
observations.why_it_matters: CI can run a clean gate in one job but publish based on repository JSON state in another job, weakening the truth ladder between live gate output and published artifacts.
observations.automation_candidate: Upload the live release closeout, batch manifest, and evidence bundle from verify-clean-release and make publish consume those artifacts and their digests.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-07: release.yml verify-clean-release now uploads separate verified-source-zip, verified-evidence-bundle, and verified-release-attestation artifacts; publish downloads those live artifacts and verifies SHA/provenance bindings with release_live_artifact_attestation instead of reading checked-in closeout JSON. Follow-up automated on 2026-05-07: review-archive in the verify job now writes tmp/live-review-archive-report.json and uploads that live report with tmp/live-source.zip instead of uploading the checked-in review-archive report.

observations.surface: learning delta scoreboard and same-eval run telemetry
observations.problem: Legacy same-eval run telemetry was incomplete for the proposal family until the remaining runs were evidence-backed backfilled or replaced.
observations.why_it_matters: Learning claims must stay closed until the same-eval family has machine-checkable typed reason, strict secondary improvement, and behavior delta digest evidence rather than only telemetry presence.
observations.automation_candidate: Run a new same-eval mechanism experiment or perform evidence-backed legacy backfill that populates the three typed telemetry fields from concrete baseline/candidate artifacts without inferring unsupported claims.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-07: retry/retry3/timeout legacy telemetry now carries typed same_eval_reason_code, strict_secondary_improvement_present, secondary_improvement_axes, and behavior_delta_digest from actual artifacts; retry2 was archived because behavior-delta and changed-files evidence were absent and is superseded by run-20260507-auto-improve-existing-telemetry-inline-same-eval-replacement. learning_delta_scoreboard now reports 5/5 full coverage while learning_claim_allowed remains false pending explicit unlock review.

observations.surface: release audit pack optional JUnit/log payload handling
observations.problem: The batch manifest now records optional JUnit/log payload policy and digests, but the closeout recipe cleans tmp before the final batch manifest and audit-pack materialization, so optional payload entries can be listed while their tmp files are unavailable.
observations.why_it_matters: JSON digests remain authoritative, but an operator who opts into portable raw pytest payloads needs a stable preservation window or staging location that survives closeout tmp cleanup.
observations.automation_candidate: Stage optional JUnit/log payloads under a non-authoritative audit payload directory or move the optional audit-pack materialization before tmp cleanup while keeping checked-in JSON as authority.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-07: full-suite JUnit/log payload defaults now use RELEASE_AUDIT_PAYLOAD_STAGING_DIR=build/release-payloads so final tmp cleanup does not force listed_unavailable; release-audit-pack exposes RELEASE_AUDIT_PACK_INCLUDE_OPTIONAL_PAYLOADS=1 for operators who explicitly want those raw payloads in the portable bundle.

observations.surface: tests/test_generated_report_contracts.py artifact freshness canonical report assertions
observations.problem: The artifact freshness finalization test pinned exact artifact_count and run_log_placeholder_count values, so adding or archiving any run-local artifact required editing unrelated numeric expectations.
observations.why_it_matters: Release contract tests should catch semantic drift in debt axes, not make every legitimate artifact inventory change look like a hand-maintained counter update.
observations.automation_candidate: Derive expected artifact and run-log placeholder counts from the checked-in manifest/report sections and keep exact assertions only for semantic zero-debt axes.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-07: the generated report contract now derives artifact_count, json_artifact_count, and run_log_placeholder_count from artifact_records and run_log_placeholders while preserving strict zero-debt assertions for envelope/schema/currentness debt.

observations.problem: Adding release-distribution-zip and release-evidence-closeout-sealed updated the Makefile surface, but the sealed closeout recipe refreshed generated index and freshness without first refreshing make-target inventory or workflow dependency planner reports.
observations.why_it_matters: The release batch can be clean while advisory observability reports still describe an older target graph, making follow-up planning and public export audits lag behind the actual runtime contract.
observations.automation_candidate: Include make-target-inventory and workflow-dependency-planner in the final closeout refresh before generated-artifact-index and artifact-freshness, and assert that ordering in Makefile static contract tests.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-07: release-evidence-closeout now refreshes make-target-inventory and workflow-dependency-planner immediately before the final generated-artifact-index/archive/freshness pass; tests assert the order so new target-surface changes are represented in final observability artifacts.

observations.problem: The tmp batch-manifest check fixture used live filesystem mtimes for freshly seeded source files, so second-precision generated_at comparisons could fail even when no source content changed.
observations.why_it_matters: Release sealing tests should validate real content and freshness behavior rather than depend on scheduler timing between fixture writes and manifest generation.
observations.automation_candidate: Freeze fixture source mtimes or use explicit ZIP metadata in tmp manifest check tests so source freshness is deterministic.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-07: release_sealing_lane now freezes seeded fixture file mtimes to a fixed past timestamp before building/checking the tmp batch manifest, preserving the digest-mismatch test while removing the mtime race.

observations.surface: mechanism-review-candidates and mutation-proposals for repeated same-eval/discard pressure
observations.problem: The active mutation proposal had narrowed to repeated_discard_runs, but inherited the full eval-stagnation run window, mixing same-eval-only runs into the next single failure-mode experiment evidence set.
observations.why_it_matters: A proposal labeled as DISCARD-only can still drive rework if its run evidence asks the next operator to reason over the broader same-eval/discard family instead of one terminal outcome path.
observations.automation_candidate: Emit signal-specific run ID groups from mechanism review and have mutation proposal selection use the group matching the selected failure_mode.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-07: mechanism eval-stagnation candidates now expose signal_run_ids for repeated_discard_runs and repeated_same_eval_after_promote, and mutation proposals select metrics/run_ids from the chosen failure_mode so the active proposal is a DISCARD-only 2-run evidence slice.

observations.surface: tests/fixtures/report_schema_samples.json and report schema sample regeneration
observations.problem: Adding the mechanism-review candidate signal_run_ids contract changed schema-derived sample fingerprints, leaving the frozen OpenVEX sample fixture stale until regeneration.
observations.why_it_matters: Release closeout can spend a full-suite cycle discovering a deterministic fixture drift that is directly implied by a schema contract edit.
observations.automation_candidate: After schema contract changes, run the schema sample regeneration helper before release closeout or add a narrower pre-closeout smoke for frozen sample drift.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-07: report_schema_samples.json was regenerated after adding signal_run_ids so report schema sample regeneration tests match the current schema fingerprint set.
